description: Baseline language capability for the VistA Evolved platform. Provides English-language UI strings, terminal prompts, VistA dialog translations, and menu translations. English is the baseline product language; all other language packs supplement but do not replace it. Full language support for additional languages requires per-language packs and distro runtime readiness.

readiness.evidenceSummary: Pack manifest exists (lang-en.json) defining English baseline. No runtime translation artifacts or string bundles exist yet. Pack lifecycle is draft.
readiness.scopeBounds: English language only. No non-English language support is covered by this capability instance. Additional languages require separate language packs and distro verification.
constrainingFactors: lang-en pack is draft — no runtime string bundles or translation files exist.
Cross-repo implementation required: distro owns VistA terminal translations, platform owns UI strings.
No other language packs are available in the current PH seed corpus.

packDependencies.rationale: English baseline is the foundational language pack. All language-support capability depends on it.

claimPosture.permittedScopeStatement: English language pack manifest has been authored as a planning artifact. No runtime language implementation exists yet.
prohibitedClaims: Must not claim 'multilingual support' based on this capability alone.
Must not claim 'language-ready' for any non-English language.
Must not claim runtime language switching capability.

visibilityPosture.rationale: Language-support capability is visible in control-plane for planning purposes but cannot be activated. Not shown to tenant admins until runtime implementation exists.

activationConstraints: lang-en pack must reach at least published lifecycle state.
Runtime string bundles must exist in platform and/or distro.
No runtime language infrastructure exists yet.
deactivationConstraints: English baseline should not be deactivated as it is the foundation for all language support.

notes: This is a global capability — not PH-market-specific. All markets require English baseline.
Additional language packs (lang-fil, lang-ko, lang-es) supplement this capability when authored and verified.
Distro L0-L5 maturity model governs language readiness depth per language.
